session.user:
Find the following files and report their paths and key content: 1. FakeRemoteGitHub class - find its file path and constructor signature 2. Any existing operation unit tests under tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/ - list them 3. The MachineCommandError class definition 4. The `machine_command` decorator definition to understand how it works 5. The `resolve_owner_repo` and `get_remote_github` functions 6. The `context_for_test` function in tests/fakes/tests/shared_context.py
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
session.assistant: I'll help you find these files and their key content. Let me start by checking the documentation index, then systematically explore the codebase.
session.tool_result:
1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)
... [22 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Now let me check the testing documentation and then search for the specific files:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: FakeGitHubIssues Dual-Comment Parameters 3→read_when: 4→ - "setting up FakeGitHubIssues in a test" 5→ - "test fails with empty comments from FakeGitHubIssues" 6→ - "choosing between comments and comments_with_urls parameters" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "passing string values to comments_with_urls parameter of FakeGitHubIssues" 9→ warning: "comments_with_urls requires IssueComment objects, not strings. Strings cause silent empty-list returns. Match the parameter to the ABC getter method your code calls." 10→ - action: "creating custom FakeGitHubIssues without passing to build_workspace_test_context" 11→ warning: "Always pass issues=issues to build_workspace_test_context when using custom FakeGitHubIssues. Without it, plan_backend operates on a different instance and metadata writes are invisible." 12→ - action: "asserting on FakeGitHubIssues.added_comments for PlannedPRBackend.add_comment()" 13→ warning: "PlannedPRBackend routes comments to PR comments (FakeGitHub.pr_comments), not issue comments (FakeGitHubIssues.added_comments). Check the correct fake when testing planned-PR comment operations." 14→ score: 6 15→--- 16→ 17→# FakeGitHubIssues Dual-Comment Parameters 18→ 19→## Why Two Comment Parameters Exist 20→ 21→<!-- Source: erk_shared/gateway/github/issues/abc.py, GitHubIssues.get_issue_comments --> 22→<!-- Source: erk_shared/gateway/github/issues/abc.py, GitHubIssues.get_issue_comments_with_urls --> 23→ 24→The `GitHubIssues` ABC exposes two distinct comment-retrieval methods: `get_issue_comments()` returns plain body strings, while `get_issue_comments_with_urls()` returns full `IssueComment` objects (with id, url, body, and author). These serve different use cases — simple body inspection vs. operations that need comment identity (reactions, updates, URL linking). 25→ 26→<!-- Source: erk_shared/gateway/github/issues/fake.py, FakeGitHubIssues.__init__ --> 27→ 28→`FakeGitHubIssues` mirrors this split with two constructor parameters: `comments` (for body strings) and `comments_with_urls` (for `IssueComment` objects). Each parameter feeds exactly one getter method. They are completely independent stores — populating one does not affect the other. 29→ 30→## The Pitfall: Silent Type Mismatch
... [45 lines omitted] ...
32→Because both parameters are dicts keyed by issue number, it's easy to pass the wrong type to the wrong parameter. The failure mode is silent: the getter returns an empty list rather than raising a type error, because it's querying the unpopulated store.

1→--- 2→title: CLI Testing Patterns 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "writing tests for erk CLI commands" 7→ - "using ErkContext.for_test()" 8→ - "testing Click commands with context" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "testing code that reads ERK_PLAN_BACKEND or other environment variables via CliRunner" 11→ warning: "CliRunner env var isolation: ambient env vars from the developer shell leak into CliRunner by default and cause intermittent test failures. Use CliRunner(env={'ERK_PLAN_BACKEND': '...'}) to override, or CliRunner(env={}) to isolate completely. Never rely on ambient env being clean. Note: mix_stderr parameter is broken in Click 8.3.1 — do not use it." 12→ score: 7 13→ - action: "renaming a user-facing string in CLI output and updating related test assertions" 14→ warning: "Test assertion lag: after renaming display strings (e.g., 'issue' → 'plan'), grep all test files for the old literal before committing. Tests using old string literals against stale snapshots will silently pass — the failure only surfaces in CI on a clean checkout." 15→ score: 6 16→ - action: "passing mix_stderr parameter to CliRunner" 17→ warning: "Click 8.3.1 CliRunner no longer accepts mix_stderr parameter. Use CliRunner() or CliRunner(env={}) instead." 18→--- 19→ 20→# CLI Testing Patterns 21→ 22→This document covers testing patterns specific to erk CLI commands that use `ErkContext` for dependency injection. 23→ 24→## Overview 25→ 26→Erk CLI commands use Click's context system (`@click.pass_context`) to receive dependencies. Testing these commands requires passing a test context via the `obj` parameter to `CliRunner.invoke()`. 27→ 28→## The ErkContext.for_test() Pattern 29→ 30→`ErkContext.for_test()` is the canonical factory method for creating test contexts with fake dependencies.
... [491 lines omitted] ...
148→### Pattern 4: Testing Error Handling 150→When testing error paths: 166→ # Assert: Command exits with error 356→- [ ] Test both success and error paths

1→"""Fake implementation of RemoteGitHub for testing. 2→ 3→Constructor-injected responses for reads, mutation tracking lists for write assertions. 4→""" 5→ 6→from dataclasses import dataclass, replace 7→ 8→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueInfo, IssueNotFound, PRReference 9→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.abc import RemoteGitHub 10→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.types import RemotePRInfo, RemotePRNotFound 11→ 12→ 13→@dataclass(frozen=True) 14→class CreatedRef: 15→ """Recorded create_ref call.""" 16→ 17→ owner: str 18→ repo: str 19→ ref: str 20→ sha: str 21→ 22→ 23→@dataclass(frozen=True) 24→class CreatedFileCommit: 25→ """Recorded create_file_commit call.""" 26→ 27→ owner: str 28→ repo: str 29→ path: str 30→ content: str

1→"""Machine command decorator for pure-JSON CLI commands. 2→ 3→Provides @machine_command for commands that: 4→- Accept JSON input from stdin (not Click params) 5→- Return structured JSON output 6→- Use frozen dataclass request types for strict validation 7→- Support --schema flag for introspection 8→ 9→Unlike @json_command (which adds --json/--schema to human commands), 10→@machine_command creates dedicated machine-only commands with no 11→Click-based input parsing. The request type IS the input schema. 12→""" 13→ 14→import json 15→from dataclasses import dataclass 16→from typing import Any 17→ 18→import click 19→ 20→from erk_shared.agentclick.dataclass_json import ( 21→ emit_json_error, 22→ emit_json_success, 23→ parse_dataclass_from_json, 24→ read_json_stdin, 25→ serialize_to_json_dict, 26→) 27→ 28→ 29→@dataclass(frozen=True) 30→class MachineCommandMeta:
... [188 lines omitted] ...
38→class MachineCommandError: 39→ """Structured error result from a machine command.""" 41→ error_type: str 59→ to_json_dict()) or a MachineCommandError. The decorator handles 79→ json.JSONDecodeError: If stdin contains invalid JSON 80→ ValueError: If stdin JSON is not an object 99→ ValueError: On validation errors (unknown fields, missing required, type mismatch) 104→def emit_machine_error(error: MachineCommandError) -> None: 105→ """Emit a structured error to stdout.""" 106→ emit_json_error(error_type=error.error_type, message=error.message) 152→ except json.JSONDecodeError as exc: 153→ emit_machine_error( 154→ MachineCommandError( 155→ error_type="invalid_json_input", 160→ except ValueError as exc: 161→ emit_machine_error( 162→ MachineCommandError( 163→ error_type="invalid_json_input", 175→ except ValueError as exc: 176→ emit_machine_error( 177→ MachineCommandError( 178→ error_type="invalid_request", 188→ if isinstance(result, MachineCommandError): 189→ emit_machine_error(result) 194→ except click.ClickException as exc: 195→ error_type_attr = getattr(exc, "error_type", None) 196→ if error_type_attr is not None: 197→ emit_machine_error( 198→ MachineCommandError( 199→ error_type=str(error_type_attr),

1→"""Shared --repo infrastructure for CLI commands. 2→ 3→Provides helpers for resolving owner/repo from either a --repo flag 4→or local git context, and constructing RemoteGitHub instances. 5→""" 6→ 7→import functools 8→ 9→import click 10→ 11→from erk.cli.ensure import UserFacingCliError 12→from erk.core.context import ErkContext, NoRepoSentinel 13→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId 14→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.abc import RemoteGitHub 15→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.real import RealRemoteGitHub 16→ 17→ 18→def resolve_owner_repo( 19→ ctx: ErkContext, 20→ *, 21→ target_repo: str | None, 22→) -> tuple[str, str]: 23→ """Resolve owner/repo from --repo flag or local git context. 24→ 25→ Args: 26→ ctx: ErkContext 27→ target_repo: Optional "owner/repo" string from --repo flag 28→ 29→ Returns: 30→ Tuple of (owner, repo_name)

1→"""Test factories for creating ErkContext instances. 2→ 3→This module provides factory functions for creating test contexts with 4→fake implementations. These are used by both erk and erk-kits tests. 5→""" 6→ 7→from __future__ import annotations 8→ 9→from pathlib import Path 10→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING 11→ 12→from erk_shared.context.context import ErkContext 13→from erk_shared.context.types import GlobalConfig, LoadedConfig, RepoContext 14→ 15→if TYPE_CHECKING: 16→ from erk.artifacts.paths import ErkPackageInfo 17→from erk_shared.core.prompt_executor import PromptExecutor 18→from erk_shared.gateway.agent_docs.abc import AgentDocs 19→from erk_shared.gateway.agent_launcher.abc import AgentLauncher 20→from erk_shared.gateway.claude_installation.abc import ClaudeInstallation 21→from erk_shared.gateway.cmux.abc import Cmux 22→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace.abc import Codespace 23→from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import Git 24→from erk_shared.gateway.github.abc import LocalGitHub 25→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues 26→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId, RepoInfo 27→from erk_shared.gateway.github_admin.abc import GitHubAdmin 28→from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.abc import Graphite 29→from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.branch_ops.abc import GraphiteBranchOps 30→from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.disabled import GraphiteDisabled

session.assistant: Perfect! Now let me get the test file names under tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: List all test files in pr operations directory

session.assistant:
Excellent! Now let me provide you with a comprehensive report of all the findings:
## Summary of File Locations and Key Content
### 1. **FakeRemoteGitHub Class** **File Path:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/fakes/gateway/remote_github.py`
**Constructor Signature:** ```python def __init__( self, *, authenticated_user: str, default_branch_name: str, default_branch_sha: str, next_pr_number: int, dispatch_run_id: str, issues: dict[int, IssueInfo] | None, issue_comments: dict[int, list[str]] | None, pr_references: dict[int, list[PRReference]] | None, comments_by_id: dict[int, str] | None = None, prs: dict[int, RemotePRInfo] | None = None, ) -> None ```
**Key Details:** - Frozen dataclass implementing `RemoteGitHub` ABC - Constructor-injected responses for read operations - Mutation tracking lists for write assertions (stored as private attributes) - Read-only properties like `created_refs`, `created_file_commits`, `created_pull_requests`, `updated_pr_bodies`, etc. for test assertions - Methods for read operations: `get_authenticated_user()`, `get_default_branch_name()`, `get_issue()`, `get_pr()`, etc. - Methods for write operations: `create_ref()`, `create_file_commit()`, `create_pull_request()`, `update_pull_request_body()`, etc.
---
### 2. **Unit Tests under tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/**
**Main test files:** - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/test_dispatch_cmd.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/test_dispatch_helpers.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/test_lifecycle_update.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/test_metadata_helpers.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/test_shared.py`
**Submit pipeline subtests (under submit_pipeline/):** - `test_capture_existing_pr_body.py` - `test_cleanup_impl_for_submit.py` - `test_commit_wip.py` - `test_core_submit_flow.py` - `test_enhance_with_graphite.py` - `test_extract_diff.py` - `test_extract_diff_and_fetch_plan_context.py` - `test_fetch_plan_context.py` - `test_finalize_pr.py` - `test_generate_description.py` - `test_graphite_first_flow.py` - `test_label_code_pr.py` - `test_link_pr_to_objective_nodes.py` - `test_prepare_state.py` - `test_run_pipeline.py`
---
### 3. **MachineCommandError Class** **File Path:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/machine_command.py`
**Definition:** ```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class MachineCommandError: """Structured error result from a machine command."""
error_type: str message: str ```
**Key Details:** - Frozen dataclass with two fields: `error_type` (string) and `message` (string) - Used to represent structured error results from machine commands - Can be returned from a machine command callback to indicate failure
---
### 4. **machine_command Decorator Definition** **File Path:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/machine_command.py`
**Decorator Signature:** ```python def machine_command( *, request_type: type, output_types: tuple[type, ...], ) -> Any: """Decorator for machine-only CLI commands with dataclass-based input.
Must be applied ABOVE @click.command in the decorator stack.
Adds --schema flag. Reads JSON from stdin, validates against request_type, and passes the constructed request to the callback. The callback receives the request as a keyword argument named 'request'.
The callback should return a result object (dataclass or object with to_json_dict()) or a MachineCommandError. The decorator handles serialization.
Args: request_type: Frozen dataclass type for input validation output_types: Result types for schema generation """ ```
**How it Works:** 1. Must be applied ABOVE `@click.command` in decorator stack 2. Automatically adds `--schema` flag for introspection 3. Reads JSON from stdin (not Click params) 4. Validates input against frozen dataclass `request_type` 5. Passes constructed request to callback as `request` kwarg 6. Callback returns result object or `MachineCommandError` 7. Handles JSON serialization/deserialization automatically 8. Supports `--schema` mode to introspect input/output types
---
### 5. **resolve_owner_repo and get_remote_github Functions** **File Path:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/repo_resolution.py`
**resolve_owner_repo Function:** ```python def resolve_owner_repo( ctx: ErkContext, *, target_repo: str | None, ) -> tuple[str, str]: """Resolve owner/repo from --repo flag or local git context.
Args: ctx: ErkContext target_repo: Optional "owner/repo" string from --repo flag
Returns: Tuple of (owner, repo_name)
Raises: UserFacingCliError: If owner/repo cannot be determined """ ```
**get_remote_github Function:** ```python def get_remote_github(ctx: ErkContext) -> RemoteGitHub: """Get or construct a RemoteGitHub from context.
Uses ctx.remote_github if provided (tests inject FakeRemoteGitHub here). Otherwise constructs RealRemoteGitHub from ctx.http_client.
Args: ctx: ErkContext with http_client and time
Returns: RemoteGitHub instance
Raises: UserFacingCliError: If no http_client is available """ ```
**Key Details:** - `resolve_owner_repo()` resolves owner/repo from either `--repo` flag or local git context - Validates format is "owner/repo" with exactly one slash - `get_remote_github()` returns test-injected `FakeRemoteGitHub` if available, otherwise constructs `RealRemoteGitHub` - Both functions raise `UserFacingCliError` on validation failures
---
### 6. **context_for_test Function** **File Path:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/fakes/tests/shared_context.py`
**Signature:** ```python def context_for_test( *, github_issues: GitHubIssues | None = None, git: Git | None = None, github: LocalGitHub | None = None, github_admin: GitHubAdmin | None = None, graphite: Graphite | None = None, claude_installation: ClaudeInstallation | None = None, agent_launcher: AgentLauncher | None = None, agent_docs: AgentDocs | None = None, prompt_executor: PromptExecutor | None = None, codespace: Codespace | None = None, cmux: Cmux | None = None, plan_store: PlanBackend | None = None, local_config: LoadedConfig | None = None, remote_github: RemoteGitHub | None = None, debug: bool = False, repo_root: Path | None = None, cwd: Path | None = None, repo_info: RepoInfo | None = None, package_info: ErkPackageInfo | None = None, ) -> ErkContext ```
**Key Details:** - Factory function for creating test contexts with optional pre-configured implementations - Provides full control over all context parameters with sensible test defaults - Returns an `ErkContext` with fake implementations for all services - Plan backend defaults to `PlannedPRBackend` unless explicitly overridden - Default paths: `repo_root=Path("/fake/repo")`, `cwd=Path("/fake/worktree")` - Automatically creates fake implementations for any unspecified dependencies: - `FakeGitHubIssues` for github_issues - `FakeGit` for git - `FakeLocalGitHub` for github - `FakeGraphite` for graphite - `FakeClaudeInstallation` for claude_installation - And many others...
**Usage Example (from docstring):** ```python >>> from tests.fakes.gateway.github_issues import FakeGitHubIssues >>> from tests.fakes.gateway.git import FakeGit >>> github = FakeGitHubIssues() >>> git_ops = FakeGit() >>> ctx = context_for_test(github_issues=github, git=git_ops, debug=True) ```
---
## Related Documentation
The documentation files at `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/cli-testing.md` and `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/fake-github-testing.md` provide comprehensive guidance on using these testing patterns and fakes in actual test implementations.
